src

A small Rust surface where one top-level module hands work to one parser leaf and returns its numeric answer.

What this is

This directory is a tiny Rust library root that exposes a parser module and a single public function, answer, which returns a u32 by calling that parser with the literal text "42".

Role in the whole

The root file is the binding point between the outer library API and the child parser directory: it publishes the module and delegates the actual conversion work to parser::parse_answer.

That delegation makes the directory cooperate as a simple pipeline, with the root fixed on orchestration and the child fixed on parsing the numeric text it receives.

Composition

src/parser

The only child page; it holds the parser leaf that accepts text and turns the fixture input into a typed number.

The directory’s internal shape is one module declaration and one exported function, so the root stays thin and the child owns the conversion step.

Seams and contracts

The main seam is the handoff from a hard-coded string in the root to the parser’s string input, and the contract on that handoff is that the text must be suitable for numeric parsing.

The parser child makes that contract explicit by parsing the input directly and using expect with the message fixture input is numeric, so success is the only supported outcome on this path.

Where it can hurt you

The sharp edge sits in the child parser: invalid text does not return a recovery value, because expect turns parse failure into a panic.

That matters to the operator of the root API as well, because answer always sends the parser the fixed string "42", so the whole path depends on that input staying within the numeric contract.
